docs.text:
• About Me

    Welcome to my site, wiki, and blog — a space where I enthusiastically share my passion for technology and recount my experiences.

    My interests revolve around architecture, patterns and principles, artificial intelligence, code-generation, etc. I am dedicated to consistently achieving elegance and simplicity in code.

  • Projects

docs.title: Disclaimer
docs.text: The information on this website represents my personal opinion. My opinion may differ with other people’s opinion and my employer’s opinion. This website is by no means related to other people nor my employer.

docs.text: This Internet site contains links to Internet sites or to web pages of third parties. I have no control of the contents or other characteristics of these sites and I am under no circumstances responsible for the contents of it.

docs.title: Blog comments
docs.text: It is allowed for registered people to post comments on articles I write. I have no control of the contents or other characteristics of these comments and I am under no circumstances responsible for the contents of it. You can contact me in order to remove certain comments.

docs.title: Link to this site
docs.text: It is permitted, without preceding written authorization of the website owner, to put a link between your site and this site.

docs.title: Purpose of using personal information
docs.text: If you transfer your personal details to me, these are incorporated in my files and are processed for the management of my member file and the offered service. Data are under no circumstances passed on without your personal authorization to third parties.

docs.title: Cookies
docs.text: To offer you an optimal service, I make use of “cookies”, which are a small piece information that is stored on your computer: these cookies can be picked up during a later visit to the same site. The cookie are not possible read by another Internet site than my website. You have the possibility in your browser to configure it that it informs you each time cookies are produced or used. Blocking cookies of my site may result in incorrect behavior of my website.

docs.title: MkDocs
docs.text: • Adding favicons to external links in MkDocs

docs.title: A fresh start
docs.text:
After a decade-long hiatus, I've made the decision to revive my blog about my passion for technology and to share my experiences. Moreover, this site serves as my personal wiki, a digital repository where I document the tools and insights integral to my daily life as a technical consultant."

I began blogging in 2004 and utilized DasBlog at that time. I created my own theme called Business which gained popularity in the blogosphere and contributed to a multi-user setup — DasBlog 1.9 released.

For a long time, I used DasBlog but eventually I switched to Wordpress

I decided to make a fresh start because much of the content from that time has become obsolete. I opted to use MkDocs to create a beautiful documentation site.

I also converted my old blog site to MkDocs which is accessible here. To facilitate the transformation, I used wordpress-export-to-markdown to have an initial head start and made some adaptations afterward.

docs.title: Adding favicons to external links in MkDocs
docs.text:
I've developed a MkDocs plugin called mkdocs-link-favicon, designed to facilitate the automatic generation of a favicon before each external link. You can observe the functionality of this plugin on this website.

This plugin is highly customizable, allowing users to use their own icons, exclude specific sites, and even choose to open external links in a new tab. For detailed information and installation instructions, please visit the GitHub repository.

docs.title: Language bar not showing on GitHub repository
docs.text:
I recently encountered an issue with my GitHub repository, mkdocs-link-favicon, where the language bar was not being generated. After some investigation, I discovered that GitHub uses a library called Linguist to create language breakdown graphs, and these graphs are recalculated every time changes are pushed to a repository. You can find more details on this process here.

The source code for my project is located in the docs folder, and unfortunately, this folder is excluded from the Linguist analysis.

To address this issue, you can override the Linguist settings by adding a .gitattributes file to your repository. More information about the possible customizations can be found here.

.gitattributes
docs/** -linguist-documentation

Warning

Note the use of the minus sign ("-") before linguist-documentation, which ensures the inclusion of all files and directories in the docs folder.

You can test these settings locally using a Docker image for Linguist. Simply navigate to your repository in PowerShell and execute the following command:

docker run -t --rm -v ${pwd}:/repo crazymax/linguist:latest

This Docker command allows you to simulate the Linguist environment locally and verify whether the language bar is being generated correctly.

docs.title: Basic example
docs.text:
docker-compose.yml
services:
  web:
    build:
    # build from Dockerfile
      context: ./Path
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    ports:
     - "5000:5000"
    volumes:
     - .:/code
  redis:
    image: redis

docs.title: Info & Stats
docs.text:
# Show installed docker version
docker version

# Show the logs of a container
docker logs <container> <image>

# Show stats of running container’s
docker stats

# Show processes of container
docker top <container>

docs.title: Images
docs.text:
# Build an image from a Dockerfile
docker build -t <image> .

# Build an image from a Dockerfile without the cache
docker build -t <image> . --no-cache

# List local images
docker images

# Delete an image
docker rmi <image>

# Remove all unused images
docker image prune

docs.title: Containers
docs.text:
# Create and run a container from an image with a custom name
docker run --name <container> <image>

# Run a container with and publish a container’s port(s) to the host.
docker run -p <host_port>:<container_port> <image>

# Run a container in the background
docker run -d <image_name>

# Start or stop an existing container
docker start|stop <container_name> (or <container-id>)

docs.title: Key Commands
docs.text:
git init : Initialize a Git repository (only required once per project) git add <file(s)> Stage code changes (for the next commit) git commit -m "...": Create a commit for the staged changes (with a message) git status : Get the current repository status (e.g. which changes are staged) git log: Output a chronologically ordered list of commits git checkout <id>: Temporarily move back to commit git revert <id>: Revert the changes of commit (by creating a new commit) git reset <id>: Undo commit's up to commit by deleting commits

git reset --hard

in Vim to quit type :wq

git branch feature-restructure git branch git checkout feature-restructure git branch -D feature-restructure // delete

git checkout -b feature-restructure // directly checkout and create a branch git add .

git checkout master git merge feature-restructure

docs.title: Systemd
docs.text:
sudo systemctl stop caddy
sudo systemctl disable caddy

docs.title: Networking
docs.text:
sudo ss -lntp | grep -E ':80|:443'
sudo netstat -tulnp | grep -E ':(80|443)'

docs.title: Upgrade packages
docs.text: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get upgrade -y

docs.title: Install SSH
docs.text:
sudo apt install openssh-server -y
sudo systemctl enable ssh
sudo systemctl start ssh
sudo systemctl status ssh

docs.title: Enable Root login for SSH
docs.text:
# Set root password
sudo passwd root
sudo -i
nano /etc/ssh/sshd_config
/etc/ssh/sshd_config
#LoginGraceTime 2m
#PermitRootLogin prohibit-password
PermitRootLogin yes
#StrictModes yes
#MaxAuthtries 6
#MaxSessions 10

sudo systemctl restart sshd

docs.title: Ollama
docs.text:
# Starts Ollama on your local system.
ollama serve

# Downloads the specified model to your system.
ollama pull <model>

docs.title: How to get access to /var/run/docker.sock
docs.text:
sudo synogroup --add docker <userid>
sudo chgrp docker /var/run/docker.sock

docs.title: Access through a shortcut
docs.text:
1. Right-click blank area of your computer desktop and select New -> Shortcut

  2. In the Create Shortcut window type

    %windir%\System32\rundll32.exe sysdm.cpl,EditEnvironmentVariables

  3. Click Next and give it a name like Environment Variables
